channel.title: ScreenLock Updates
channel.description: Auto-update feed for ScreenLock — a Mac utility that locks keyboard, mouse, and trackpad while apps keep running.

item.title: Version 0.3.2
item.description:
What's new in 0.3.2

  • Cursor freeze actually freezes now. When the "Mouse movement (cursor)" toggle is on, the cursor stops moving on screen while locked. Previously, mouseMoved events were being swallowed for apps but the WindowServer was still moving the cursor visually in response to mouse hardware. Now we call CGAssociateMouseAndMouseCursorPosition to disconnect the cursor from the mouse while locked, and re-associate on unlock.

item.title: Version 0.3.1
item.description:
What's new in 0.3.1

  • Media keys are now locked too. Volume up/down/mute, brightness up/down, play/pause, next/previous track, Mission Control and Launchpad keys are all blocked when the Keyboard category is enabled. Previously they slipped through because macOS delivers them as a different event type.

item.title: Version 0.3.0
item.description:
What's new in 0.3.0

  • PIN unlock — alternate to the hotkey. Type your 4–8 digit PIN on the keyboard while locked; digits stay invisible.
  • Auto-unlock timer — pick 15 / 30 / 60 / 120 minutes from the menu; ScreenLock unlocks itself when time's up.
  • Per-category lock toggles — independently lock keyboard, mouse clicks, mouse movement, scrolling, and trackpad gestures.
  • Custom hotkey recorder — set any combo with at least one of ⌘ ⌥ ⌃.
  • Signed by KERIGAMI LLC, notarized by Apple, auto-updates enabled.
